segments.text: Lesson 22.

translations.zh-Hans: 第22课。

segments.text: and Progress.

translations.zh-Hans: 知识与进步。

segments.text: First listen and then answer the following question.

translations.zh-Hans: 先听录音，然后回答下面的问题。

segments.text: In what two areas have people made no progress at all?

translations.zh-Hans: 人们在哪两个方面毫无进步？

segments.text: Why does the idea of progress loom so large in the modern world?

translations.zh-Hans: 为什么进步这一观念在现代世界显得如此重要？

segments.text: Surely because progress of a particular kind is actually taking place around us and is becoming more and more manifest.

translations.zh-Hans: 当然是因为某种特定的进步确实正在我们周围发生，而且日益明显。

segments.text: Although mankind has undergone no general improvement in intelligence or morality, it has made extraordinary progress in the accumulation of knowledge.

translations.zh-Hans: 尽管人类的智力或道德总体上没有提高，但在知识积累方面却取得了非凡进步。

segments.text: began to increase as soon as the thoughts of one individual could be communicated to another by means of speech.

translations.zh-Hans: 一旦一个人的思想能通过语言传达给另一个人，知识便开始增长。

segments.text: With the invention of writing, a great advance was made for knowledge could then be not only communicated but also stored.

translations.zh-Hans: 文字发明后，人类取得了巨大进步，因为知识不仅可以传播，还可以储存。

segments.text: Libraries made education possible, and education in its turn added to libraries.

translations.zh-Hans: 图书馆使教育成为可能，而教育反过来又丰富了图书馆。

segments.text: The growth of knowledge followed a kind of compound interest law, which was greatly enhanced by the invention of printing.

translations.zh-Hans: 知识增长遵循一种复利规律，印刷术的发明大大增强了这种增长。

segments.text: All this was comparatively slow until, with the coming of science, the tempo was suddenly raised.

translations.zh-Hans: 这一切原本相对缓慢，直到科学出现，速度突然加快。

segments.text: Then knowledge began to be accumulated according to a systematic plan.

translations.zh-Hans: 此后，知识开始按照系统计划积累。

segments.text: The trickle became a stream.

translations.zh-Hans: 涓涓细流变成了河流。

segments.text: The stream has now become a torrent.

translations.zh-Hans: 如今，河流已变成奔腾的洪流。

segments.text: Moreover, as soon as new knowledge is acquired, it is now turned to practical account.

translations.zh-Hans: 此外，新知识一经获得，便立即被用于实践。

segments.text: What is called modern civilization is not the result of a balanced development of all man's nature, but of accumulated knowledge applied to practical life.

translations.zh-Hans: 所谓现代文明，并非人性各方面均衡发展的结果，而是积累的知识应用于实际生活的结果。

segments.text: The problem now facing humanity is, what is going to be done with all this knowledge?

translations.zh-Hans: 如今摆在人类面前的问题是：要拿这一切知识怎么办？

segments.text: As is so often pointed out, is a two-edged weapon which can be used equally for good or evil.

translations.zh-Hans: 正如人们常指出的，知识是一把双刃剑，既可用于善，也可用于恶。

segments.text: It is now being used indifferently for both.

translations.zh-Hans: 现在，它正不加区别地被用于两者。

segments.text: Could any spectacle, for instance, be more grimly whimsical than that of gunners using science to shatter men's bodies, while, close at hand, surgeons use it to restore them?

translations.zh-Hans: 例如，还有什么景象比这更冷酷荒诞：炮手用科学摧毁人的身体，近旁的外科医生却用科学修复人的身体？

segments.text: We have to ask ourselves very seriously what will happen if this two-fold use of knowledge, with its ever-increasing power, continues.

translations.zh-Hans: 如果知识这种善恶并用的方式带着不断增强的力量继续下去，我们必须极其严肃地追问将会发生什么。
